matches.x: Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though [it be] but a man's covenant, yet [if it be] confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.
And though I have [the gift of] prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.
And though I bestow all my goods to feed [the poor], and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.

The Great Commandment By Elder Joseph B. Wirthlin Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles When we reach out to assist the least of Heavenly Father’s children, we do it unto Him. Brethren and sisters, I would like to ask one very important question: What quality defines us best as members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints? Today I would like to speak about the answer to this…

In praising charity, the Apostle Paul declared that charity "thinketh no evil; rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth" (1 Corinthians 13:5-6; see also Moroni 7:45). Similarly, the prophet Moroni warned those who "despise the works of the Lord" (Mormon 9:26). (Dallin H. Oaks, Pure in Heart, p.57)
And eternal righteousness among the holy ones. And thou shalt be inscribed in the book of life. He rejoiceth over her, and she rejoiceth over him, Love the fear of the Lord,
[He is] the Rock, his work [is] perfect: for all his ways [are] judgment: a God of truth and without iniquity, just and right [is] he.
None calleth for justice, nor [any] pleadeth for truth: they trust in vanity, and speak lies; they conceive mischief, and bring forth iniquity.
And they will deceive every one his neighbour, and will not speak the truth: they have taught their tongue to speak lies, [and] weary themselves to commit iniquity.

In his great discourse on charity, the Apostle Paul chided the Corinthians about looking back to their younger, immature years. He then urged them to accept the responsibilities of maturity. Said Paul: "When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things" (1 Cor. 13:11). In the following chapter he admonished…

“For we know in part, and we prophesy in part. “But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away. “When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things. “For now we see through a glass darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am…

In his great discourse on charity, the Apostle Paul chided the Corinthians about looking back to their younger, immature years. He then urged them to accept the responsibilities of maturity. Said Paul: "When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things" (1 Cor. 13:11). FOR it came to pass after I had desired to know the things that my father had seen, and believing that the Lord was able to make them known unto me, as I sat pondering in mine heart I was caught away…
And it had come to pass that my father spake unto her, saying: I know that I am a visionary man; for if I had not seen the things of God in a vision I should not have known the goodness of God, but…
Wherefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we [him] no more.

Now, my son, I would not that ye should think that I know these things of myself, but it is the Spirit of God which is in me which maketh these things known unto me; for if I had not been born of God…
If we believe not, [yet] he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.

Charity is the crowning virtue, "the end of the commandment" (1 Tim. 1:5); "And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity." (1 Cor. 13:13.) (Bruce R. Mc Conkie, Mormon Doctrine, 2d ed., p.121)

And if you have not faith, hope, and charity, you can do nothing.
